steps.title: Identify the chemical contrast
steps.content: Red ocher contains iron oxide; plant-based pigments contain high carbon. The test showed the beads' pigment: no iron but high carbon—exactly the signature of plant-based pigments, not ocher.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: This signature proves the beads used plant-based pigments rather than ocher. A directly states this conclusion. B and C introduce unsupported claims (about brightness and ocher's scarcity), while D overgeneralizes to historical primacy the passage never addresses. That matches choice A — A is correct.
